seo.title: CAPIT | Plug Wells. Mint Tokens. Track Progress.
seo.description: Track verified U.S. well-plugging records, explore state-by-state progress, and review CAPIT mint activity with transparent methodology and source context.

hero.eyebrow: Public well-plugging data with transparent on-chain reporting
hero.headline: Plug Wells. Mint Tokens.
hero.description: CAPIT brings together public well-plugging records, state-by-state reporting, and CAPIT protocol activity in one place. Explore the national snapshot, compare states, and review the methodology behind every published update.
hero.trustNote: CAPIT publishes informational content and protocol materials. Please review the methodology, disclosures, and contract details before interacting with any wallet or token feature.

image.alt: CAPIT worker and plugged well illustration

snapshot.heading: National Snapshot
snapshot.intro: Start with the big picture. CAPIT highlights verified public records, recent publishing activity, state comparisons, and protocol-linked mint activity in a single view.

educationalCards.title: What Is a Plugged Well?
educationalCards.description: A plugged well is a well that a source agency records as plugged, sealed, abandoned and plugged, or an equivalent state-specific status. CAPIT should always show the exact source definition used in the current dataset view.

educationalCards.title: Why Does Plugging Wells Matter?
educationalCards.description: Plugging can reduce leakage risk, limit fluid migration pathways, and support site restoration. CAPIT explains those benefits carefully and links them back to the source behind the claim.

educationalCards.title: How Many Wells Remain?
educationalCards.description: Counts vary by source, definition, and publication date. CAPIT labels whether a figure refers to unplugged orphaned wells, broader inactive-well estimates, or a source-specific inventory.
educationalCards.imageAlt: Map showing inactive wells across the United States

methodologyStrip.heading: Source-linked. Time-stamped. Revision-aware.
methodologyStrip.body: Every CAPIT summary should point back to source logic, verification status, and last-updated timestamps. Public records can change. CAPIT should make those changes visible.
methodologyStrip.label: Read Methodology
